role: Global stylesheet that maps corpus design tokens to app-specific aliases and implements the visual language—living atmosphere, shared kit primitives, tldraw node cards, port nubs, HUD chrome, and panel feeds—for the canvas interface.
capabilities: Maps legacy app.css variables onto corpus design-system tokens
Defines a warm gold aurora atmosphere plus film-grain texture behind the canvas
Provides reduced-motion-aware ambient animations (aurora drift, entrance stagger, breathing presence)
Implements shared kit primitives: section headings, lane headers, badges, surfaces, empty states
Styles tldraw node cards with status-driven borders, output clamping, and transient flash
Styles input/output port nubs with hover affordances for wiring
Styles HUD toolbar, buttons, notices, run-error pill, presence dial, and mode selector
Styles activity feed, history/version groups, and address-help panels
Overrides tldraw background transparency and respects prefers-reduced-motion
substance_why: The visible head alone contains thousands of lines defining token aliases, atmospheric effects, animations, a shared UI kit, node cards, port nubs, HUD, activity/history/versions panels, and tldraw overrides, with extensive design-rule commentary.
interactions_present: Hover/focus color transitions on buttons, actionable surfaces, port nubs, and mode selector
Cursor changes (pointer for buttons/surfaces, crosshair for port nubs)
Collapsible lane headers and history group heads via button hover
Presence dial breathing animation and canvas-running energized aurora
Entrance stagger animations for chrome regions
Transient node-flash animation on newly added nodes
Reduced-motion media query disables aurora, entrance, and breathe animations
interactions_missing: No :focus-visible or keyboard-focus styles for buttons, lane headers, or port nubs
No explicit disabled, active, or loading states for toolbar buttons
No JS event wiring—the CSS references backend classes/endpoints but does not implement them
No responsive layout breakpoints or mobile adaptations
No explicit ARIA expanded/collapsed indicators for collapsible headers
performance_risks: Full-viewport fixed pseudo-elements with large blur filters and radial gradients composited on every frame
Continuous opacity/transform/filter animations on body::before and multiple UI elements
Full-screen SVG noise overlay with fixed positioning and high z-index
Heavy box-shadow glows layered across many components
Unscoped global rules and !important tldraw overrides increase specificity wars and recalc
Multiple simultaneous breathing/pulsing animations may cause GPU contention
reuse_why: The visual concepts, token mapping, and component vocabulary are valuable, but the monolithic global-CSS form must be split into scoped modules or a design-system library during redesign.
decomposition_hints: Extract root token aliases into a dedicated tokens/variables file
Extract living atmosphere (aurora, grain, reduced motion) into a theme/atmosphere module
Extract tldraw canvas overrides into a canvas-specific stylesheet
Extract shared kit primitives into a kit/component primitives stylesheet or CSS-in-JS library
Extract node-card, port-nub, and inspector form styles into node/editor modules
Extract HUD, toolbar, presence, and mode styles into a chrome/hud module
Extract activity, history, versions, and address-help panel styles into feed/panel modules
Extract all keyframes into a shared motion/animation module
